assertions.expectation: Identifies PDB as the most likely/common cause of stuck upgrades
assertions.reason: Response clearly states 'Check PodDisruptionBudgets (Most Likely Cause)' and identifies it as the primary issue to investigate first

assertions.expectation: Provides kubectl command to check PDBs (kubectl get pdb --all-namespaces or similar)
assertions.reason: Provides 'kubectl get pdb -A -o wide' command to check PDBs across all namespaces

assertions.expectation: Provides commands to identify which pods are blocking the drain
assertions.reason: Multiple commands provided: checking pending pods, bare pods without ownerReferences, pods on specific nodes, and monitoring draining status

assertions.expectation: Includes resource constraint diagnosis (capacity issues, pending pods)
assertions.reason: Section 2 specifically covers resource constraints with commands to check pending pods, node capacity, and scheduling failures

assertions.expectation: Provides a fix for restrictive PDBs (patch or temporary adjustment)
assertions.reason: Provides kubectl patch command to temporarily adjust PDB with maxUnavailable: '33%'

assertions.expectation: Mentions that GKE has a PDB timeout (up to 1 hour) after which it may force-drain, and that GKE sends notifications when eviction is blocked by PDB
assertions.reason: While it mentions 'GKE only waits 1 hour for pod eviction during surge upgrades', it doesn't specifically mention PDB timeout or that GKE sends notifications when eviction is blocked by PDB

assertions.expectation: Includes guidance on surge upgrade settings as a potential fix
assertions.reason: Provides gcloud command to adjust max-surge-upgrade and max-unavailable-upgrade settings

assertions.expectation: Mentions webhook issues as a possible cause
assertions.reason: Response does not mention webhooks or admission controllers as potential causes of stuck upgrades

assertions.expectation: Includes validation steps to confirm the fix is working
assertions.reason: Provides monitoring commands with 'watch' and verification steps in the 'After Upgrade Completes' section

assertions.expectation: Mentions GKE disruption-event notifications (POD_PDB_VIOLATION) that alert when eviction is blocked by PDB, available via Cloud Logging or Pub/Sub
assertions.reason: Response does not mention POD_PDB_VIOLATION events, Cloud Logging, or Pub/Sub notifications for PDB-blocked evictions
